title: Second Day of the Pause

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A second day of fragile relief where progress and loss ran side by side: the Iran–Israel pause held and US–Iran talks continued through Pakistan as crude slipped below $90 and chip-led equities ticked up, while the Mindanao quake toll climbed to 45, Russia's drones killed eight on a bus in Donetsk, the DRC's Bundibugyo-Ebola outbreak passed 90 deaths, and World Cup staff and fans were turned back at the US border — offset by a computer-designed vaccine clearing its first human trial, a habitable-zone super-Earth confirmed 10.7 light-years out, and platypuses breeding again in a Sydney river after fifty years.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano, vibraphone (VCSL), RolandTR909

lines: Day two of the quiet and the guns are still down, talks crossing borders by phone,
oil dips under ninety, the chip stocks come back, the screen ticks a careful green.
But there's an adviser in Tehran with a hand on the dial, saying one move and the sky opens up,
so we breathe like the breath could be borrowed, and hold what calm we have, in between.
Mindanao counts forty-five now, the boats reaching towns the roads couldn't find,
a bus in Donetsk, eight gone to a drone, and the Congo keeps burying its own.
Still a vaccine no hand drew passed its first trial, a new world found ten light-years out,
and a river near Sydney has platypus young — fifty years and the soft news comes home.
It's the second day of the pause, not a promise, just a door held ajar,
loss in one column, mercy the other, the ledger refusing to close.
Take the day at its weight, neither all-clear nor all-lost,
the kind of calm you stand inside slow, the way an open question goes.
